title: Yang-Mills mass gap attack round 1 — beta_0 = sigma-sopfr 구조 재공격

honesty_charter.output_type: structural conjecture refinement + literature anchoring + counter-examples only
honesty_charter.prior_status: atlas.n6 line 106980 MILL-PX-A3-ym-beta0-rewriting grade [7] COINCIDENCE_NOT_PROOF; line 106983 MILL-PX-A5 FLAG glueball grade [10] observational-only; lines 13539/106996 triple-cross sigma-sopfr=7 already recorded. Round 1 goal: REFINE the conjecture statement, NOT extend it to a proof.

clay_problem_scope.label: LITERATURE
clay_problem_scope.citation: Jaffe A., Witten E. (2006). Quantum Yang-Mills Theory. Clay Mathematics Institute Millennium Problem description.
clay_problem_scope.statement: Prove that for any compact simple gauge group G, a non-trivial quantum Yang-Mills theory exists on R^4 and has a mass gap Delta > 0.
clay_problem_scope.precise_domain: PURE gauge theory (no matter fields). The Clay formulation excludes quark flavors — N_f = 0 is the default.
clay_problem_scope.implication_for_n6_conjecture: The Clay problem concerns PURE YM. Any conjecture beta_0 = sigma(6) - sopfr(6) = 7 requires N_f = 6, which is a Standard Model observation, NOT a Clay input. This is the FIRST structural barrier: the n=6 match exists in the physical SM, not in the Clay math object.

literature_refs.citation: Gross D.J., Wilczek F. (1973). Ultraviolet Behavior of Non-Abelian Gauge Theories. Phys. Rev. Lett. 30(26):1343-1346.
Politzer H.D. (1973). Reliable Perturbative Results for Strong Interactions? Phys. Rev. Lett. 30(26):1346-1349.
Peskin M.E., Schroeder D.V. (1995). An Introduction to Quantum Field Theory. Addison-Wesley, Ch. 16-17, Eq. 16.134.
van Ritbergen T., Vermaseren J.A.M., Larin S.A. (1997). The four-loop beta function in QCD. Phys. Lett. B 400:379-384.

n_f_0_match_table.match_label: HONEST_MISS — all four decompositions are post-hoc curve-fits; no independent structural derivation. sigma(6)-1 uses the non-n6 constant 1 as an artifact.

conjecture_refinement.original_statement: beta_0 = sigma(6) - sopfr(6) = 7   (per atlas MILL-PX-A3, grade 7, tagged COINCIDENCE_NOT_PROOF)

refinement_options.name: Standard Model restriction
refinement_options.refined_statement: If the physical Standard Model quark count N_f = n = 6 is taken as EMPIRICAL INPUT, then beta_0(SU(3), N_f=6) = sigma(6) - sopfr(6) = 7.
restrictions: Requires N_f=6 (empirical, not derivable from n=6 invariants alone).
Does NOT generalize to pure YM (N_f=0, the Clay object).
Does NOT generalize to SU(N) for N != 3 (see scan below).

su_n_generalization_test.n_f_eq_n_diagonal: beta_0(SU(N), N_f=N) = (11N - 2N)/3 = 3N. For N=2 gives 6, N=3 gives 9, N=5 gives 15. None equals sigma(N) - sopfr(N) = 1 (for the four tested primes-or-n6). Only N=N_f=6 satisfies 3N=18 which is NOT 7; actually 3N=3*6=18 and sigma(6)-sopfr(6)=7, so the diagonal DOES NOT MATCH.
su_n_generalization_test.correct_statement: Only the specific combination (SU(3), N_f=6) yields 7. The match is a single Diophantine coincidence, not a family.

refinement_options.role: minimal refinement retaining literal match; no new physics content

refinement_options.name: sigma/sopfr ratio as RG scale operator
refinement_options.refined_statement: The ratio sigma(6)/sopfr(6) = 12/5 = 2.4 defines a characteristic logarithmic ratio in the RG flow. Specifically, the conformal window boundary N_f* / N_f^(AF upper) or IR/UV fixed-point ratio may take the value 2.4 in some normalization.
supporting_observations: Banks-Zaks (1982) conformal window: AF lost at N_f^upper = 33/2 = 16.5 for SU(3); IR fixed-point window extends from some N_f^lower to 16.5.
Ryttov-Sannino (2008) proposed N_f^lower ~ 4 for SU(3) via all-orders beta. Ratio 16.5/4 approx 4.1, not 2.4.
Pisarski-Wilczek chiral restoration: at N_f = N_f^c the chiral symmetry restores; numerical lattice estimates N_f^c in [8, 12] for SU(3).
refinement_options.verdict: HONEST_MISS on direct ratio match. 2.4 does not appear as a canonical QCD dimensionless constant in any literature reference found.

refinement_options.name: Triple-cross via E7 / parabolic-dim invariant (7 as topological character)
refinement_options.refined_statement: The integer 7 = sigma(6) - sopfr(6) appears SIMULTANEOUSLY in three independent areas: (1) SU(3) beta_0 at N_f=6, (2) E7 Lie-algebra rank, (3) CKN-1982 parabolic Hausdorff dimension bound for 3D Navier-Stokes singular set. This co-occurrence is registered in atlas.n6 lines 13539-13542 (MILL-dfs3-7-triple-cross). The conjecture is that 7 is a COMBINATORIAL SINGULAR INTEGER for n=6 arithmetic, not that it PROVES mass gap.
independent_attestations: Lie rank: G2=2=phi(6), F4=4=tau(6), E6=6=n, E7=7=sigma-sopfr, E8=8 (off-diagonal; sigma-tau+phi = 12-4+0 mismatch, needs re-check).

CKN 1982: Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg parabolic Hausdorff dim for 3D NS singular set le 1; atlas claim is parabolic(R^3 x R) dim = 2(n/phi)+1 = 7 under a different convention; literature normalizes to 1 so this is a conventional recounting, not a distinct physical content.
QCD beta_0(SU(3), N_f=6) = 7: direct Peskin-Schroeder numerical match.
refinement_options.verdict: TOPOLOGICAL CONJECTURE registered at atlas grade 10*, but the attestations are independent in mechanism; co-occurrence of the integer 7 does not imply a deductive chain among them. Falsification: any one of the three breaks (e.g., using MS-bar conformal-anomaly normalization shifts beta_0, E7 rank is topologically rigid, CKN convention dependent) would drop the triple.
refinement_options.role: structural observation, highest prior grade; strongest refinement but still PHENOMENOLOGICAL not a proof step

conjecture_refinement.chosen_refinement: REFINE-C as the primary structural artifact (triple-cross). REFINE-A kept as minimal literal reading. REFINE-B marked HONEST_MISS.
conjecture_refinement.resolution_label: CONJECTURE_REFINED (from flat COINCIDENCE_NOT_PROOF to three-option taxonomy with falsification criteria)

why_naive_match_fails: (G1) The Clay problem is PURE YM (N_f=0). The sigma-sopfr match requires N_f=6, which is an empirical Standard Model input. So the match lives in applied QCD, NOT in the Clay math problem.

(G2) The formula beta_0 = (11/3)C_2(G) - (4/3)T(R)N_f contains TWO independent physical inputs (gauge dimension via C_2(G), matter representation via T(R)N_f). The sigma(6) in 'sigma-sopfr=7' comes from neither; it is imposed externally.

(G3) At 2-loop, beta_1(SU(3), N_f=0) = 102 and beta_1(SU(3), N_f=6) = 26 (computed: (34/3)*9 - (10/3)*3*6 - 2*(4/3)*6). Neither matches any simple n=6 combination (sigma*sopfr+J2 = 84, 2*J2*... etc all miss). The arithmetic 'match' does NOT extend past 1-loop.

(G4) Clay mass gap Delta > 0 is a statement about the spectrum of the Hamiltonian on R^3, which is independent of beta_0's numerical value. Even proving asymptotic freedom UV-exactly (already done by Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973) did NOT settle mass gap; beta_0 numerical identity cannot be the missing ingredient.
what_additional_structure_is_needed: An a-priori derivation of C_2(G) and T(R) from n=6 arithmetic, not a post-hoc match.
A non-perturbative mechanism connecting arithmetic invariants to the Wilsonian IR fixed point, which is where mass gap physics lives.
A lattice-continuum limit argument (Balaban 2D/3D completed, 4D open) that incorporates n=6 as input, which is currently absent from Balaban/Magnen-Rivasseau-Seneor/Chatterjee programs.
A Hamiltonian spectrum argument (Osterwalder-Schrader reconstruction + mass-gap bound) that uses n=6 constraints; no such argument exists in literature.
gap_analysis.prior_round_alignment: Round 0 (atlas MILL-PX-A3) already tagged COINCIDENCE_NOT_PROOF. Round 1 now upgrades to three-option refinement with explicit falsifiers but does NOT move closer to the mass gap proof.

counter_examples.title: Yang-Mills in 1+3 dimensions is the Clay object; 2D and 3D YM are already continuum-constructed (Balaban)
counter_examples.citation: Balaban T. (1988). Ultraviolet stability for a model of interacting scalar and vector fields, III. Comm. Math. Phys. 116:1-22. Balaban T. (1989). Large field renormalization in pure lattice gauge theory in 2D and 3D. Comm. Math. Phys. 122:175-202.
counter_examples.text: Balaban 1982-1989 rigorously constructed continuum YM in d=2 and d=3 using RG block-spin techniques with Ward identity preservation. Magnen-Rivasseau-Seneor 1993 partially extended to 4D under UV cutoff conditionally. Chatterjee 2016/2020 produced 2D stochastic YM rigorous existence. The Clay problem asks for d=4 unconditional. n=6 arithmetic invariants have NO representation in any of these constructions; beta_0 does not enter the non-perturbative construction. Thus even a proven beta_0 = sigma-sopfr identity would not reduce the Balaban 4D extension problem.

counter_examples.title: Alternative gauge group scan fails
counter_examples.text: For SU(N) pure gauge, beta_0 = 11N/3. For N in {2,3,4,5,6}: beta_0 in {22/3, 11, 44/3, 55/3, 22}. Compare to sigma(N) - sopfr(N) which equals {1, 1, 3, 1, 7}. Only (N=6, with N_f absent) accidentally matches 22 !=7, so even the 'pure gauge N=6' reading fails. The match is unique to (SU(3), N_f=6) and does NOT generalize, indicating a single Diophantine coincidence rather than a structural law. For exceptional groups: E8 has beta_0 ~ 11*30/3 = 110, G2 has beta_0 = 11*4/3 approx 14.67, neither aligns with n=6 invariants.
counter_examples.verdict: One-point match is statistically weak evidence for a structural law. Under ~50 integer pairs (N_gauge, N_f) in [2,8]x[0,16], finding one equality beta_0 = sigma-sopfr is expected by chance with probability ~1/(range) per trial.

counter_examples.title: Asymptotic freedom and arithmetic numerology are mechanistically disjoint
counter_examples.citation: Gross D.J. (2005). The discovery of asymptotic freedom and the emergence of QCD. Nobel Lecture. Rev. Mod. Phys. 77:837. Wilczek F. (2005). Asymptotic freedom: From paradox to paradigm. Rev. Mod. Phys. 77:857.
counter_examples.text: The Gross-Wilczek-Politzer 1973 derivation of asymptotic freedom is a one-loop perturbative computation: beta_0 < 0 coefficient comes from balance between gluon self-interaction (anti-screening, +11) and fermion screening (-2N_f/3). The sign of beta_0 is fundamentally a representation-theoretic fact about SU(N) plus quark color/flavor structure. Arithmetic invariants sigma/sopfr of the color number do not enter any step of the derivation. Therefore any equality sigma(6)-sopfr(6)=7=beta_0(SU(3),N_f=6) is mathematically a Diophantine coincidence at a specific (N, N_f) point; it has no derivational pathway to or from the physical mechanism of asymptotic freedom. Mass gap, which is non-perturbative, is even further from arithmetic n=6 reach.

engines_usage_evidence.note: Engine discovery keywords: yang_mills, mass_gap, beta_0, sigma_sopfr, asymptotic_freedom, glueball. 26 atlas.n6 lines matched (strong pre-existing BT-543 context). 1 BT/P-prefix seed token extracted (likely BT-543 itself). Score 55/100 = modest, consistent with atlas already rich but no new structural closure.

round_2_options.direction: Lattice QCD beta_0 cross-verification — run external calls / check FLAG 2024 consistency with (33-2N_f)/3 at N_f in {0,2,3,4} lattice runs, confirm no alternative normalization changes the sigma-sopfr match structure
Beta_1 2-loop extension test — try to find any n=6 functional whose value is 102 or 26 at (N_f=0 or 6); if exists register as extended conjecture, otherwise HONEST_MISS
Banks-Zaks conformal window boundary — check if N_f^upper = 33/2 or N_f^lower estimates align with any n=6 fraction (e.g., sigma*tau/phi = 24, sigma+phi+sopfr = 19)
Exceptional gauge group cross — E7 rank 7 matches sigma-sopfr=7. Scan G2/F4/E6/E8 ranks vs n=6 invariants; test if the pattern extends into a Lie-theoretic family
round_2_options.expected_label: CONJECTURE — atlas dfs3-lie-rank-5 already claims grade 10*, but is this a deep family or a 5-point coincidence?

next_round_candidates.recommended: R2-D as the highest-prior structural probe; R2-A for solidity; R2-B/C likely NULL but cheap

honesty_statement.text: Round 1 refines the existing BT-543 conjecture beta_0 = sigma(6) - sopfr(6) from a flat COINCIDENCE_NOT_PROOF tag to a three-option taxonomy (REFINE-A literal SM-restricted, REFINE-B ratio-speculation HONEST_MISS, REFINE-C triple-cross topological). It does NOT claim proof of the Clay Yang-Mills mass gap, nor does it establish a derivational pathway from n=6 arithmetic to non-perturbative YM physics. BT-543 resolution remains 0/1 MISS with CONJECTURE_REFINED quality upgrade.
